topic: Tidal N2 (larger lunar elliptic semi-diurnal) period
our_argument_summary: N2 (12.66 hours) is a semi-diurnal lunar constituent caused by the moon's elliptical orbit (varying distance). The dome model places the moon at ~2,534 km with no derived orbital eccentricity. At that altitude above the 20,015 km disc, tidal force is sharply localized — no far-side bulge. The author has never published a tidal amplitude or spatial pattern calculation using dome geometry. Verdict: Self-Contradicted.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No tidal validation code exists in the dome's repository. The tidal constituent WINs (045-051) were added in V51.0 as static text claims. No monitor.py domain handles tidal periods. No pull_data.py fetch retrieves tidal observations. The raw-text/02-wins.txt file (V50.6 extraction) does not list any tidal WINs, confirming they are V51.0 additions with no monitoring infrastructure.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Not applicable — no monitoring code exists for this WIN. The tidal constituent WINs appear as static claims with no automated verification pipeline.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None identified. The claim is a simple period-match assertion with no tolerance logic or adaptive verification.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our review correctly identifies that tidal WINs have no monitoring infrastructure.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain, data fetch, or comparison logic exists for tidal constituents. V50.6 raw text (02-wins.txt) doesn't list any tidal WINs — they were added in V51.0 as static claims. Searched all raw-text files for N2, tidal, and constituent references in monitoring code — none found.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: N2 (12.66 hours) has been catalogued since Doodson (1921) and is a fundamental constituent in standard tidal harmonic analysis. Standard tidal theory explains N2 as the modulation of the principal semidiurnal lunar tide (M2) by the moon's orbital eccentricity — when the moon is nearer, M2 amplitude increases; when farther, it decreases. This amplitude modulation produces a beat frequency at the N2 period. The dome model adopts this known period and relabels the mechanism as dome-compatible without changing any numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: N2's period has been known and tabulated for over a century (Doodson 1921 catalogued 389 harmonic constituents). The dome model claimed it as a WIN in V51.0 (April 2026), over 100 years after the observation was first published. The dome's 'prediction' is the known measured value restated.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation connects dome geometry (disc radius 20,015 km, moon altitude 2,534 km, firmament function H(r)) to N2's period. N2 = 12.66 hours arises from the beat between M2 and the lunar perigee cycle in standard tidal theory: 1/N2 = 1/M2 - 1/Mf where Mf is the lunar fortnightly constituent (~13.66 days). This requires the moon to have an elliptical orbit with well-defined eccentricity — a parameter the dome model has never specified. No formula on the dome site traces N2 from dome-specific parameters.

holes_found.description: Cross-reference error: detail_evidence says 'See Section 4.5.4' but Section 4.5.4 is 'Solar Diameter: 50% Variation Through the Day' — unrelated to tidal patterns. The correct reference is Section 4.5.2 ('Tidal Pattern: One Spike vs. Two Bulges'). This error has been flagged in every previous tidal WIN review (045, 046, 049, 050) and persists across all five tidal WINs. A dome defender clicking this link lands on solar diameter analysis, undermining reader confidence in our internal consistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'Section 4.5.4' to 'Section 4.5.2' in the detail_evidence for WIN-051 (and all other unfixed tidal WINs). The HTML anchor href='#part4b' points to the Part 4.5 container, which is correct, but the displayed section number '4.5.4' is wrong.

holes_found.description: N2 requires lunar orbital eccentricity — a stronger argument than we're making. N2 is specifically caused by the moon's ELLIPTICAL orbit: when the moon is at perigee (closer), M2 amplitude increases; at apogee (farther), it decreases. This amplitude modulation at the anomalistic month period creates the N2 constituent. The dome model has never specified an elliptical orbit for its local moon, nor derived the moon's eccentricity from dome geometry. Our current text mentions 'no derived orbital eccentricity' but buries it — this should be the LEAD argument for N2 specifically, since it distinguishes N2 from M2/S2 (which just need a distant moon) and from K1/O1 (which need orbital motion). N2 demands the moon have a specific elliptical orbit with specific eccentricity — the most geometrically demanding of all five tidal WINs.
holes_found.recommendation: Restructure detail_evidence to lead with the eccentricity argument: 'N2 exists specifically because the moon has an elliptical orbit with eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549. The dome model has never specified an orbital eccentricity for its local moon at 2,534 km altitude, nor derived one from dome geometry. Without eccentricity, N2 has no physical origin in the dome framework — it is the one tidal constituent that cannot be hand-waved as a simple consequence of a moon existing.'

holes_found.description: The detail_claim is extremely terse: 'Tidal N2 period matches dome model.' While consistent with the other tidal WINs, it would be more informative to include the specific period value (12.66 hours) for reader clarity.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand detail_claim to: 'The N2 (larger lunar elliptic semi-diurnal) tidal constituent period of 12.66 hours matches the dome model prediction.'

holes_found.description: Unlike the diurnal WINs (049, 050), the 'two-bulge' language IS technically correct for N2 since it is semi-diurnal. However, the finding text 'dome's local moon cannot produce the observed two-bulge tidal pattern' is generic — it's identical across all five tidal WINs. For N2 specifically, the stronger and more precise critique is about the missing eccentricity. A finding that's identical across five WINs signals copy-paste rather than individual analysis.
holes_found.recommendation: Differentiate N2's finding from the generic tidal template. Suggested: 'Period is trivial (lunar elliptical); dome's local moon has no derived orbital eccentricity — N2's physical origin is absent from dome geometry.'

stronger_arguments.description: N2 has a unique falsification angle: its AMPLITUDE RATIO to M2 directly encodes the moon's orbital eccentricity. The ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19, which corresponds to the moon's eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549 (the relationship is approximately N2/M2 ≈ 3e for small eccentricities). If the dome model cannot derive e = 0.0549 from its geometry, it cannot predict the N2/M2 ratio, which means it cannot predict N2's amplitude — only parrot its period. This is the sharpest argument against claiming N2 as a dome prediction: the period alone is meaningless without the amplitude ratio, and the amplitude ratio encodes precisely the orbital mechanics the dome model rejects.
sources: Pugh & Woodworth (2014) Sea-Level Science, Cambridge University Press, Chapter 4

stronger_arguments.description: N2 interacts with M2 to produce the fortnightly spring-neap tidal modulation. The observed spring-neap cycle (M2+N2 constructive interference near perigee, M2-N2 destructive interference near apogee) has been measured at every coastal tide gauge worldwide. On the dome, a local moon at 2,534 km with no specified eccentricity has no mechanism to produce this fortnightly modulation. If the dome claims N2, it must also explain why tide gauges show systematic fortnightly amplitude variations that correlate with the standard moon's perigee/apogee cycle.
sources: Foreman (1977) Manual of Tidal Observations Analysis and Prediction

kernel_of_truth.description: N2 is a real and significant tidal constituent — the third-largest semidiurnal component after M2 and S2, with typical open-ocean amplitudes of 2-6 cm. The dome model correctly identifies its period (12.66 hours) and recognizes that a complete tidal theory must account for it. Including N2 shows awareness of tidal harmonic structure beyond the basic M2/S2 pair.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Correctly stating a period catalogued in 1921 is bibliography, not prediction. N2 is uniquely demanding because its very existence requires the moon to have an elliptical orbit with specific eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549. The dome model has never specified this eccentricity, never derived it from dome geometry, and never calculated N2's amplitude (which is the physically meaningful test). Furthermore, the dome's OPEN-007 explicitly concedes that 'the moon's orbital mechanics have no working dome-native explanation' — the very mechanics that generate N2. Claiming N2 as a confirmed prediction while acknowledging that lunar orbital mechanics are an unresolved open problem is self-contradictory.

deeper_analysis:
WIN-051 completes the five-WIN tidal constituent group (045-051). The group shares systemic issues: trivial period matching, no dome-geometric derivation, no monitoring infrastructure, post-hoc adoption of century-old data, and a recurring cross-reference error (4.5.4 → 4.5.2). However, N2 has a distinctive feature that our review underexploits. Unlike M2 (which just needs the moon to exist), S2 (which needs the sun), K1/O1 (which need orbital motion), N2 specifically requires the moon's orbit to be ELLIPTICAL with a known eccentricity. This makes N2 the most demanding test in the tidal group — if the dome model cannot derive e ≈ 0.0549, it cannot claim to predict N2 in any meaningful sense. The N2/M2 amplitude ratio directly encodes this eccentricity and could be used as a quantitative test.

The two-bulge critique is technically valid for N2 (being semi-diurnal), unlike for K1/O1 where previous reviews correctly flagged the conflation of diurnal and semidiurnal physics. However, the finding text is generic across all five tidal WINs, which signals copy-paste rather than tailored analysis. A more precise finding for N2 would emphasize the eccentricity requirement.

The dome's OPEN-007 is particularly relevant to N2. The dome explicitly concedes that 'the moon's orbital mechanics have no working dome-native explanation.' N2 is the tidal constituent most directly dependent on orbital mechanics (specifically eccentricity). Claiming N2 as a confirmed prediction while acknowledging the underlying mechanics are unsolved is a textbook self-contradiction — arguably cleaner than the generic 'local moon can't produce two bulges' argument.

Overall, our verdict holds. The Self-Contradicted category is appropriate: the dome's own OPEN-007 concession and the absence of any eccentricity derivation make the contradiction internal to the dome model, not just an external-data refutation. Minor edits recommended: fix the cross-reference, lead with eccentricity, differentiate from generic tidal template.

citations_verified: Doodson (1921) — DOI 10.1098/rspa.1921.0088 verified in WIN-045/049/050 reviews as correct DOI for 'The Harmonic Development of the Tide-Generating Potential' in Proc. Royal Society A, Vol. 100, No. 704, pp. 305-329
citations_unchecked: N2 period 12.66 hours — standard value, consistent across NOAA, IHO, and textbook sources; did not re-derive but confident in this standard value
Section 4.5.4/4.5.2 cross-reference — confirmed wrong in all previous tidal WIN reviews
N2/M2 amplitude ratio ≈ 0.19 and its relationship to eccentricity — based on standard tidal theory, not independently verified against primary source in this review
